best_time_to_visit: Early morning (04:30-07:00) for joggers and tai chi groups, or late afternoon (16:00-18:00) for pleasant light and cooler temperatures

description.en:
## Overview

Lumpini Park is Bangkok's largest and most beloved green space — 57.6 hectares (142 acres) of lawns, shaded paths, and lakes right in the heart of the Silom-Sathorn business district. Named after the Buddha's birthplace in Nepal, it serves as the city's escape valve: office workers jog the perimeter track at dawn, families picnic under rain trees on weekends, and tourists come to see the park's famous resident water monitor lizards basking along the lakeshores.

## History

The park was created in the 1920s by King Rama VI on royal land that once housed a horticultural fair. It opened to the public in 1925 and has been Bangkok's flagship urban park ever since. During World War II it briefly served as a Japanese military camp. A major renovation completed in 2022 upgraded pathways, lighting, and restroom facilities throughout the park.

## What to See

The central lake is the visual centerpiece — you can rent swan-shaped pedal boats for 40 THB per 30 minutes and glide past clusters of water monitors that can reach up to 2 meters in length. Despite their size, the lizards are used to humans and generally ignore visitors. The park has a well-maintained 2.5 km jogging loop, outdoor fitness stations, basketball and tennis courts, and a public library. Between December and February, free outdoor concerts by the Bangkok Symphony Orchestra take place on Sunday evenings.

## Practical Info

The most convenient access is via Lumphini MRT (exit 1) or Sala Daeng BTS (5-minute walk). Cycling is permitted only between 10:00 and 15:00. Smoking, alcohol, and dogs are all prohibited. Restrooms and drinking water stations are available throughout. Bring mosquito repellent for lakeside areas, especially at dusk.
description.nl: Grote groene ruimte in het centrum van Bangkok perfect voor joggen en ontspanning

tips: Visit at dawn to see locals practicing tai chi and yoga — the park is near-empty and coolest before 07:00
Rent a swan pedal boat (40 THB/30 min) for close-up views of the massive water monitor lizards along the lake
Catch a free Bangkok Symphony Orchestra concert on Sunday evenings between December and February
